title: Umbilicus mirus
taxonomy: Umbilicus mirus is classified as a species of succulent flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae. The genus Umbilicus belongs to the order Saxifragales. It is native to South Africa, specifically the Western Cape. The classification of Umbilicus mirus places it within the kingdom Plantae. This classification is based on its morphology and genetic characteristics.

intro: Umbilicus mirus, also known as the "greater purslane," is a succulent plant species belonging to the family Crassulaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Umbilicus mirus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Umbilicus mirus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or ointment, and is used to treat various conditions, including skin irritations and gastrointestinal issues.
